content.text:
This project is overall done well but there are some issues and some unimplemented tasks i found so act as a senior engineer and fix them all #: 1
  Feature: QR code generation for fulfillment checkpoints
  Status: ❌ Missing
  Details: Prompt says "OTP or QR codes" — only OTP (6-digit numeric) is
    implemented. No QR code rendering (no qrcode crate or equivalent).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  #: 2
  Feature: Offline-first / Service Worker
  Status: ❌ Missing
  Details: Prompt says "offline-first REST APIs" — there is no service worker,
  no
    offline cache, no IndexedDB sync. The app requires the backend to be
  running.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  #: 3
  Feature: Frontend route protection/guards
  Status: ❌ Missing
  Details: Role-gated pages are accessible via URL — role checks happen inside
    page components but there are no route guards preventing navigation. An
    unauthenticated user can visit /admin and see an empty page instead of being

    redirected.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  #: 4
  Feature: Notification badge in navbar
  Status: ❌ Missing
  Details: NavBar doesn't show unread notification count. Notifications only
    visible on Profile page.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  #: 5
  Feature: Favorites display as project cards
  Status: ⚠️  Partial
  Details: Favorites page shows a list of project IDs as links, not rich project

    cards with details.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  #: 6
  Feature: Spending updates public ledger reflecting refunds "immediately after
    approval"
  Status: ⚠️  Partial
  Details: Refunds update the database correctly, but there's no real-time/push
    mechanism — the UI requires a page refresh to see updated figures.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  #: 7
  Feature: Role assignment beyond bootstrap
  Status: ⚠️  Partial
  Details: Admin can assign roles, but there's no UI for user registration as
    ProjectManager or FinanceReviewer — register always creates Supporter role.
    Admin must manually promote users.

1	# Community Giving & Fund Transparency Portal
2	
3	An offline-first platform for local nonprofits to run project-based donation drives,
4	disclose spending, and close the loop with supporter engagement and accountable
5	fulfillment proof.
6	
7	## Architecture
8	
9	```
10	repo/
11	  common/       Shared Rust types (DTOs, enums) used by both server and frontend
12	  backend/      Axum REST API server with SQLite persistence
13	  frontend/     Leptos CSR (WebAssembly) single-page application
14	  database/     Runtime SQLite data directory
15	```
16	
17	### Roles
18	
19	| Role             | Capabilities |
20	|------------------|-------------|
21	| Supporter        | Browse projects, donate, comment, favorite, follow updates |
22	| Project Manager  | Create projects, post updates, record expenses, manage fulfillment |
23	| Finance Reviewer | Verify receipts, approve/reject expense disclosures and refunds |
24	| Administrator    | All of the above plus moderation config, webhooks, ops log, user management |
25	
26	Registration always assigns the **Supporter** role; higher roles are granted by
27	an Administrator via `POST /api/admin/assign-role`.
28	
29	**Bootstrap (first admin):** On a fresh deployment with no administrators, any
30	authenticated user can call `POST /api/admin/bootstrap` with their password to
31	promote themselves to Administrator. This endpoint is disabled once an admin exists.
32	
33	### Security
34	
35	- **Authentication** — Argon2id password hashing, HMAC-SHA256 signed session
36	  tokens (24 h TTL).
37	- **Replay protection** — Every mutating request (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) must
38	  carry a fresh `X-Nonce` header consumed from `GET /api/auth/nonce` (5-minute
39	  validity). Missing or reused nonces are rejected.
40	- **Rate limiting** — 60 requests per minute per session token.
41	- **IDOR prevention** — Project Managers can only modify their own projects.
42	  `require_project_owner()` is enforced on updates, expenses, fulfillments,
43	  ticket responses.
44	- **Data privacy** — Expenses, receipts, tickets, and fulfillment records are
45	  behind authentication with project-scoped authorization. PII is masked in CSV
46	  exports.
47	- **Encryption at rest** — Reviewer notes on expenses and receipt file data are
48	  encrypted with AES-256-GCM (nonce || ciphertext) using a host-managed key
49	  stored outside the repository tree.
50	- **SQL hardening** — All queries use parameterized placeholders; no user input
51	  is ever interpolated into SQL strings.
52	- **Content moderation** — Configurable sensitive-word filter and optional
53	  pre-moderation queue.
54	- **Sensitive actions** — Project unpublish, comment removal, and refund
55	  approval all require password re-entry.
56	
57	### Offline Payments
58	
59	Donations are recorded as local cash / check / card-terminal entries. Refunds
60	create negative reversal records that require Finance Reviewer approval (with
61	password confirmation). Unapproved reversals are excluded from all accounting
62	totals.
63	
64	### Fulfillment Verification
65	
66	Three-checkpoint flow (arrival, start, end) using 6-digit OTP codes with
67	10-minute expiry. Time-consistency rules: start must be within 2 hours of
68	arrival; end must follow start. Completion generates a SHA-256 tamper-evident
69	service record downloadable as proof.
70	
71	### Webhooks
72	
73	On-prem only — URLs are validated by parsing the host and checking against
74	RFC 1918 / loopback / link-local IP ranges. Payloads are HMAC-SHA256 signed,
75	retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff, and every delivery attempt is
76	logged.
77	
78	### Event Instrumentation
79	
80	Unified local event model (impressions, clicks, dwell time, sessions) with
81	3-second deduplication, burst detection (>20 events / 10 s flagged), and
82	data-quality metrics on the admin KPI console.
83	
84	## Environment Variables
85	
86	| Variable | Default | Description |
87	|----------|---------|-------------|
88	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY_PATH` | `/var/lib/fund_transparency/encryption.key` | AES-256 encryption key file (auto-generated if missing) |
89	| `HMAC_KEY_PATH` | `/var/lib/fund_transparency/hmac.key` | HMAC signing secret file (auto-generated if missing) |
90	| `DB_PATH` | `data/fund_transparency.db` | SQLite database file path |
91	| `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` | `http://localhost:8080,http://127.0.0.1:8080` | Comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins |
92	
93	Keys are stored **outside the repository tree** by default. For local
94	development, override to a writable path:
95	
96	```bash
97	export ENCRYPTION_KEY_PATH=./data/encryption.key
98	export HMAC_KEY_PATH=./data/hmac.key
99	```
100	
101	The `data/` directory is in `.gitignore` and must never be committed.
102	
103	## Prerequisites
104	
105	- Rust stable (1.75+)
106	- `wasm32-unknown-unknown` target: `rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown`
107	- [Trunk](https://trunkrs.dev/) for the WASM frontend: `cargo install trunk`
108	
109	## Building
110	
111	```bash
112	# Build the backend
113	cargo build -p server --release
114	
115	# Build the frontend (WASM)
116	cargo build -p web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release
117	# Or use Trunk for a dev server with hot reload:
118	cd frontend && trunk serve
119	```
120	
121	## Running
122	
123	```bash
124	# Start the API server (port 3000)
125	cargo run -p server
126	
127	# In another terminal, start the frontend dev server (port 8080, proxies /api to :3000)
128	cd frontend && trunk serve
129	```
130	
131	The server creates `data/fund_transparency.db`, `data/hmac.key`, and
132	`data/encryption.key` on first run.
133	
134	## Testing
135	
136	```bash
137	cargo test -p server
138	```
139	
140	The test suite covers registration role lock, auth token lifecycle, nonce
141	replay rejection, refund ownership, IDOR checks, webhook URL validation, and
142	accounting integrity.
143	
144	## API Overview
145	
146	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
147	|--------|------|------|-------------|
148	| POST | `/api/auth/register` | No | Register (always Supporter) |
149	| POST | `/api/auth/login` | No | Login, returns signed token |
150	| GET  | `/api/auth/nonce` | No | Fresh replay-protection nonce |
151	| GET  | `/api/projects` | No | List/filter/search projects |
152	| GET  | `/api/projects/:id` | No | Project detail with budget breakdown |
153	| POST | `/api/donations` | Token | Record offline donation |
154	| POST | `/api/donations/refund` | Token | Request refund (donor only) |
155	| POST | `/api/donations/refund/approve` | Token+pw | Approve/reject refund (finance) |
156	| POST | `/api/comments` | Token | Post comment (moderation-aware) |
157	| POST | `/api/comments/:id/delete` | Token+pw | Delete comment (admin only) |
158	| POST | `/api/receipts/upload` | Token | Upload receipt (PDF/JPG/PNG, 10 MB max, SHA-256 dedup) |
159	| POST | `/api/receipts/review` | Token | Verify/reject receipt (finance) |
160	| POST | `/api/fulfillments` | Token | Create fulfillment record |
161	| POST | `/api/fulfillments/code` | Token | Generate checkpoint OTP |
162	| POST | `/api/fulfillments/checkpoint` | Token | Record checkpoint with OTP |
163	| GET  | `/api/admin/stats` | Token | Dashboard KPIs with date range |
164	| GET  | `/api/admin/export/csv` | Token | Masked donation CSV export |
165

1	use server::{auth, crypto, db, middleware, routes, AppState};
2	
3	use axum::{
4	    middleware as axum_mw,
5	    routing::{delete, get, post, put},
6	    Router,
7	};
8	use std::sync::Arc;
9	use tower_http::cors::CorsLayer;
10	use tower_http::services::ServeDir;
11	
12	#[tokio::main]
13	async fn main() {
14	    let db_path = std::env::var("DB_PATH")
15	        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "data/fund_transparency.db".to_string());
16	    if let Some(parent) = std::path::Path::new(&db_path).parent() {
17	        std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).ok();
18	    }
19	    let db = db::init_db(&db_path);
20	
21	    // Host-managed key paths — outside repo tree by default
22	    let hmac_path = std::env::var("HMAC_KEY_PATH")
23	        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "/var/lib/fund_transparency/hmac.key".to_string());
24	    let hmac_secret = load_or_create_secret(&hmac_path);
25	    let encryption_key = crypto::load_or_create_key().unwrap_or_else(|e| {
26	        eprintln!("FATAL: {}", e);
27	        std::process::exit(1);
28	    });
29	    let rate_limiter = middleware::RateLimitState::new(60, 60); // 60 requests per 60 seconds
30	
31	    let state = Arc::new(AppState {
32	        db,
33	        hmac_secret,
34	        encryption_key,
35	        rate_limiter,
36	    });
37	
38	    // CORS: restrict to configured origins; fall back to same-origin only in production.
39	    let allowed_origins = std::env::var("CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS")
40	        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "http://localhost:8080,http://127.0.0.1:8080".to_string());
41	    let origins: Vec<axum::http::HeaderValue> = allowed_origins
42	        .split(',')
43	        .filter_map(|o| o.trim().parse().ok())
44	        .collect();
45	    let cors = CorsLayer::new()
46	        .allow_origin(origins)
47	        .allow_methods([
48	            axum::http::Method::GET,
49	            axum::http::Method::POST,
50	            axum::http::Method::PUT,
51	            axum::http::Method::DELETE,
52	            axum::http::Method::OPTIONS,
53	        ])
54	        .allow_headers([
55	            axum::http::header::CONTENT_TYPE,
56	            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,
57	            axum::http::HeaderName::from_static("x-nonce"),
58	        ]);
59	
60	    // Public routes (no auth required) – minimal surface
61	    let public_routes = Router::new()
62	        .route("/api/auth/register", post(routes::auth_routes::register))
63	        .route("/api/auth/login", post(routes::auth_routes::login))
64	        .route("/api/auth/nonce", get(routes::auth_routes::get_nonce))
65	        .route("/api/projects", get(routes::projects::list_projects))
66	        .route("/api/projects/{id}", get(routes::projects::get_project))
67	        .route("/api/projects/{id}/comments", get(routes::comments::list_comments))
68	        // Event tracking (allows anonymous)
69	        .route("/api/events/track", post(routes::events::track_event));
70

160	fn load_or_create_secret(path: &str) -> Vec<u8> {
161	    use std::path::Path;
162	    let p = Path::new(path);
163	    if p.exists() {
164	        std::fs::read(p).expect("Failed to read HMAC key")
165	    } else {
166	        use rand::Rng;
167	        let secret: Vec<u8> = (0..64).map(|_| rand::thread_rng().gen()).collect();
168	        if let Some(parent) = p.parent() {
169	            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).ok();
170	        }
171	        std::fs::write(p, &secret).expect("Failed to write HMAC key");
172	        secret
173	    }
174	}

**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/frontend/src/api.rs`

**Nonce Pattern (Lines 6-17):**
- Every mutating request fetches fresh nonce from `GET /api/auth/nonce`
- Nonce passed in `X-Nonce` header on POST/PUT/DELETE

**Auth/Login Endpoints (Lines 97-130):**

- `register(email, password, display_name, role)` -> `AuthResponse`
- `get_me(token)` -> `UserProfile`

**Notifications (Lines 315-340):**
- `list_notifications(token)` -> `Vec<Notification>`
- `mark_notification_read(token, notif_id)`
- `mark_all_read(token)`

- `subscribe(token, project_id)`
- `unsubscribe(token, project_id)`

### 4. Backend Architecture

#### Main Server Setup
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/main.rs`

**Server Configuration (Lines 12-157):**
- Listens on `0.0.0.0:3000`
- Database initialization: SQLite at `DB_PATH` (default: `data/fund_transparency.db`)
- HMAC secret loaded from `/var/lib/fund_transparency/hmac.key` (host-managed, outside repo)
- Encryption key loaded from `/var/lib/fund_transparency/encryption.key`
- Rate limiter: 60 requests/60 seconds per session token
- CORS configured from `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` env

**Route Organization:**
- **Public routes** (Lines 61-69): register, login, nonce, list projects, list comments, event tracking
- **Auth routes** (Lines 72-137): All routes requiring authentication, applied with middleware

**Middleware Stack (Lines 142-150):**
1. Nonce replay protection (POST/PUT/DELETE only)
2. Rate limiting (per token or IP)
3. CORS layer
4. Auth extraction (Bearer token)

#### Authentication & Sessions
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/auth.rs`

**Password Hashing (Lines 1-30):**
- Algorithm: Argon2id with random salt (Line 12-19)
- Verification: Constant-time comparison (Line 22-29)

**Session Tokens (Lines 33-84):**
- Format: `base64(user_id|expires_epoch).hex_signature`
- Signing: HMAC-SHA256 over base64 payload
- TTL: 24 hours (86400 seconds)
- Validation checks signature, expiry, and decoding

**Nonce Generation (Lines 87-91):**
- Random 16-byte hex string
- Stored with 5-minute expiry in database
- Consumed on use (prevents replay)

#### Registration & Role Assignment
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/auth_routes.rs`

**Registration Endpoint (Lines 10-63):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/auth/register`
- **Requirements:** Email + password (min 8 chars)
- **Role Lock:** Always assigns `Role::Supporter` (Line 46)
- **Higher roles assigned only by admins via `/api/admin/assign-role`**
- **Logs:** ops_log records with masked email (Line 60)

**Login Endpoint (Lines 65-94):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/auth/login`
- **Verification:** Argon2 password check
- **Response:** Signed token + UserProfile

**DND Settings (Lines 118-134):**
- **Path:** `PUT /api/auth/dnd`
- **Fields:** `dnd_start`, `dnd_end`, `timezone`
- **Persisted:** In users table

#### Role Assignment
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/admin.rs` (Lines 117-166)

**Assign Role Endpoint:**
- **Path:** `POST /api/admin/assign-role`
- **Auth:** Administrator only
- **Sensitive:** Requires password verification + 2-step confirmation token
- **Validation:** Target role parsed from string (Role::from_str)
- **Logging:** ops_log records the role change with target user info

**Bootstrap Admin (Lines 169-211):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/admin/bootstrap`
- **Condition:** Only works if zero administrators exist
- **Purpose:** First user can self-promote to admin with password
- **Disabled once any admin exists**

#### Middleware & Authorization
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/middleware.rs`

**Rate Limiting (Lines 50-81):**
- Per-session token or per-IP (fallback)
- 60 requests per 60 seconds
- Uses sliding window with `Instant` tracking

**Auth Extraction (Lines 84-102):**
- Reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
- Validates token signature & expiry
- Injects `AuthUser { user_id }` into request extensions
- Silently skips if no token (routes enforce auth separately)

**Nonce Enforcement (Lines 104-131):**
- Skips GET/OPTIONS (idempotent)
- Requires `X-Nonce` header on POST/PUT/DELETE
- Calls `db::consume_nonce()` to check validity & prevent replay
- Returns 409 CONFLICT if used/expired, 400 BAD_REQUEST if missing

**Role Checking (Lines 148-169):**
- `require_role(db, user_id, allowed_roles)` -> Returns UserProfile or 403 FORBIDDEN
- `require_project_owner(db, user_id, project_id)` -> Checks manager_id or admin bypass
- Administrator always passes project owner check (Line 160-161)

#### QR/OTP Implementation
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/fulfillment.rs`

**Generate Checkpoint Code (Lines 72-111):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/fulfillments/code`
- **Generates:** Random 6-digit OTP code
- **Expiry:** 10 minutes (Line 96-100)
- **Stores in DB:** `checkpoint_codes` table with code_id, fulfillment_id, checkpoint, code, expires_at, consumed flag
- **Ownership:** Project owner check via fulfillment's project_id

**Record Checkpoint (Lines 113-210):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/fulfillments/checkpoint`
- **Code Validation:** `db::consume_checkpoint_code()` verifies & consumes
- **Time-Consistency Rules:**
  - Arrival: No checks
  - Start: Must be within 2 hours of arrival (Lines 150-165)
  - End: Must be strictly after start (Lines 167-183)
- **Service Proof Generation (Lines 189-203):**
  - When End checkpoint recorded, generates SHA-256 hash of: `id|project_id|arrival|start|end`
  - Stored in `service_record_hash` for tampering detection

**Service Proof Download (Lines 212-243):**
- **Path:** `GET /api/fulfillments/{id}/proof`
- **Output:** `ServiceProof` struct with all timestamps + hash

#### Refund Handling
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/donations.rs`

**Request Refund (Lines 106-160):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/donations/refund`
- **Creates:** Negative reversal record (is_reversal=1, reversal_of=original_id)
- **Ownership Check:** Only original donor can request (Line 126)
- **Status:** Pending approval (reversal_approved=NULL)

**Approve Refund (Lines 162-216):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/donations/refund/approve`
- **Auth:** FinanceReviewer | Administrator
- **Sensitive:** Password verification + 2-step confirmation
- **Updates:** reversal_approved field (true=approved, false=rejected)
- **Excluded from Accounting:** Unapproved reversals don't affect totals

**Pending Refunds Endpoint:**
- **Path:** `GET /api/donations/refund/pending`
- Fetches all reversal records with `reversal_approved IS NULL`

#### Notification Endpoints
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/notifications.rs`

**List Notifications (Lines 13-19):**
- **Path:** `GET /api/notifications`
- **Auth:** Token required
- **Returns:** `Vec<Notification>` for user_id from auth

**Mark Single as Read (Lines 21-32):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/notifications/{id}/read`
- **Updates:** is_read=1 for that notification

**Mark All as Read (Lines 34-44):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/notifications/read-all`
- **Updates:** All notifications for user to is_read=1

**Notification Structure (from common):**
- `id`, `title`, `body`, `is_read`, `is_deferred`, `created_at`
- Stored in `notifications` table (Lines 124-132 in db.rs)

#### Database Schema
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/db.rs` (Lines 15-230)

**Key Tables:**
1. **users** (Lines 18-28): id, email, display_name, password_hash, role, dnd_start, dnd_end, timezone, created_at
2. **donations** (Lines 49-61): id, pledge_number, project_id, donor_id, amount_cents, payment_method, is_reversal, reversal_of, reversal_approved, budget_line_id
3. **fulfillments** (Lines 174-183): id, project_id, arrival_at, start_at, end_at, is_complete, service_record_hash
4. **checkpoint_codes** (Lines 186-193): id, fulfillment_id, checkpoint, code, expires_at, consumed
5. **notifications** (Lines 124-132): id, user_id, title, body, is_read, is_deferred, created_at
6. **nonces** (Lines 143-146): nonce, expires_at
7. **receipts** (Lines 149-161): id, expense_id, file_name, file_type, file_size, file_data (BLOB), sha256_fingerprint, status, rejection_reason, reviewer_id
8. **expenses** (Lines 63-74): id, project_id, budget_line_id, amount_cents, description, receipt_data, disclosure_status, reviewer_id, reviewer_note
9. **ops_log** (Lines 134-141): id, actor_id, actor_name, action, detail, created_at

**Encryption:** Reviewer notes & receipt file_data encrypted with AES-256-GCM (key from host)

#### Crypto Implementation
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/crypto.rs`

**Key Management (Lines 9-47):**
- Loaded from `ENCRYPTION_KEY_PATH` env (default: `/var/lib/fund_transparency/encryption.key`)
- Auto-generated if missing (32 random bytes for AES-256)
- Stored outside repository tree on host

**Encryption Functions (Lines 50-77):**
- `encrypt(plaintext, key)` -> base64(nonce || ciphertext)
- `encrypt_bytes(plaintext, key)` -> nonce || ciphertext

**Decryption Functions (Lines 80-109):**
- `decrypt_bytes(encrypted, key)` -> plaintext bytes
- `decrypt(encoded, key)` -> plaintext string
- Format: First 12 bytes are nonce, rest is ciphertext (AES-256-GCM)

---

### 5. Offline Capabilities

**Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED**

**Evidence:**
1. **No Service Worker:** No sw.js, service worker registration, or manifest.json found
2. **No Web App Manifest:** No manifest.json for PWA configuration
3. **Frontend Index (index.html, Lines 1-10):** Minimal HTML, no manifest link
4. **No Offline Cache Strategy:** Leptos CSR without gloo-indexeddb or similar
5. **No Offline Data Persistence:** Only localStorage for auth token and current_user
6. **No Cache Busting:** Trunk.toml (Lines 1-12) has no cache config

**What IS Persisted:**
- **localStorage:**
  - `auth_token` (String)
  - `current_user` (UserProfile JSON)
- **sessionStorage:**
  - `analytics_session_id` (UUID)

**Architecture:** Offline-first NOT prioritized. Platform requires live API connectivity for:
- Project data fetching
- Donation submission
- Notification fetching
- Role/permission checks
- Fulfillment checkpoint codes

- `require_project_owner(db, user_id, project_id)` -> Checks manager_id or admin bypass

#### QR/OTP Implementation
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/fulfillment.rs`

**Generate Checkpoint Code (Lines 72-111):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/fulfillments/code`
- **Generates:** Random 6-digit OTP code
- **Expiry:** 10 minutes (Line 96-100)
- **Stores in DB:** `checkpoint_codes` table with code_id, fulfillment_id, checkpoint, code, expires_at, consumed flag
- **Ownership:** Project owner check via fulfillment's project_id

**Record Checkpoint (Lines 113-210):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/fulfillments/checkpoint`
- **Code Validation:** `db::consume_checkpoint_code()` verifies & consumes
- **Time-Consistency Rules:**
  - Arrival: No checks
  - Start: Must be within 2 hours of arrival (Lines 150-165)
  - End: Must be strictly after start (Lines 167-183)
- **Service Proof Generation (Lines 189-203):**
  - When End checkpoint recorded, generates SHA-256 hash of: `id|project_id|arrival|start|end`
  - Stored in `service_record_hash` for tampering detection

**Service Proof Download (Lines 212-243):**
- **Path:** `GET /api/fulfillments/{id}/proof`
- **Output:** `ServiceProof` struct with all timestamps + hash

#### Refund Handling
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/donations.rs`

**Request Refund (Lines 106-160):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/donations/refund`
- **Creates:** Negative reversal record (is_reversal=1, reversal_of=original_id)
- **Ownership Check:** Only original donor can request (Line 126)
- **Status:** Pending approval (reversal_approved=NULL)

**Approve Refund (Lines 162-216):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/donations/refund/approve`
- **Auth:** FinanceReviewer | Administrator
- **Sensitive:** Password verification + 2-step confirmation
- **Updates:** reversal_approved field (true=approved, false=rejected)
- **Excluded from Accounting:** Unapproved reversals don't affect totals

**Pending Refunds Endpoint:**
- **Path:** `GET /api/donations/refund/pending`
- Fetches all reversal records with `reversal_approved IS NULL`

#### Notification Endpoints
**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/routes/notifications.rs`

**List Notifications (Lines 13-19):**
- **Path:** `GET /api/notifications`
- **Auth:** Token required
- **Returns:** `Vec<Notification>` for user_id from auth

**Mark Single as Read (Lines 21-32):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/notifications/{id}/read`
- **Updates:** is_read=1 for that notification

**Mark All as Read (Lines 34-44):**
- **Path:** `POST /api/notifications/read-all`
- **Updates:** All notifications for user to is_read=1

- Stored in `notifications` table (Lines 124-132 in db.rs)

**File:** `/home/eren/Documents/task4/Fund_Transparency/repo/backend/src/crypto.rs`

**Key Management (Lines 9-47):**
- Loaded from `ENCRYPTION_KEY_PATH` env (default: `/var/lib/fund_transparency/encryption.key`)
- Auto-generated if missing (32 random bytes for AES-256)
- Stored outside repository tree on host

**Encryption Functions (Lines 50-77):**
- `encrypt(plaintext, key)` -> base64(nonce || ciphertext)
- `encrypt_bytes(plaintext, key)` -> nonce || ciphertext

**Decryption Functions (Lines 80-109):**
- `decrypt_bytes(encrypted, key)` -> plaintext bytes
- `decrypt(encoded, key)` -> plaintext string
- Format: First 12 bytes are nonce, rest is ciphertext (AES-256-GCM)

### 5. Offline Capabilities

**Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED**

**Evidence:**
1. **No Service Worker:** No sw.js, service worker registration, or manifest.json found
2. **No Web App Manifest:** No manifest.json for PWA configuration
3. **Frontend Index (index.html, Lines 1-10):** Minimal HTML, no manifest link
4. **No Offline Cache Strategy:** Leptos CSR without gloo-indexeddb or similar
5. **No Offline Data Persistence:** Only localStorage for auth token and current_user
6. **No Cache Busting:** Trunk.toml (Lines 1-12) has no cache config

**What IS Persisted:**
- **localStorage:**
  - `auth_token` (String)
  - `current_user` (UserProfile JSON)
- **sessionStorage:**
  - `analytics_session_id` (UUID)

**Architecture:** Offline-first NOT prioritized. Platform requires live API connectivity for:
- Project data fetching
- Donation submission
- Notification fetching
- Role/permission checks
- Fulfillment checkpoint codes

Both backend and frontend compile successfully. Here's a summary of all 7 fixes implemented:

---

### Summary of Changes

**#1 - QR Code Generation for Fulfillment Checkpoints**
- Added `qrcode = "0.14"` to `backend/Cargo.toml`
- Added `qr_code_svg: Option<String>` field to `CheckpointCodeResponse` in `common/src/lib.rs`
- Backend `generate_code` now renders a QR code SVG encoding the OTP code (`backend/src/routes/fulfillment.rs`)
- Frontend displays the QR code SVG inline when a checkpoint code is generated (`frontend/src/pages/fulfillment.rs`)

**#2 - Offline-first / Service Worker**
- Created `frontend/static/sw.js` with network-first strategy for API (GET) and cache-first for static assets
- Created `frontend/static/manifest.json` for PWA support
- Updated `frontend/index.html` with SW registration, manifest link, and Trunk copy directives

**#3 - Frontend Route Protection/Guards**
- Created `frontend/src/components/route_guard.rs` with `AuthGuard` and `RoleGuard` components
- Wrapped all protected routes in `frontend/src/main.rs`: `/donate`, `/profile` require auth; `/admin`, `/finance`, `/dashboard`, `/staff`, `/fulfillment` require specific roles
- Unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login`; unauthorized users see a permission error

**#4 - Notification Badge in Navbar**
- NavBar now fetches unread notification count on load and auto-refreshes every 30s (`frontend/src/components/nav.rs`)
- Unread count displayed as a red badge next to "My Profile" link
- Added `.notif-badge` CSS styling

**#5 - Favorites Display as Project Cards**
- Added `list_favorite_projects` function in `backend/src/db.rs` that joins favorites with full project data
- Added `/api/favorites/projects` endpoint in backend
- Added `list_favorite_projects` API call in `frontend/src/api.rs`
- Profile page now renders favorites as `ProjectCard` components in a grid instead of plain ID links

**#6 - Spending Updates Reflecting Refunds Immediately**
- Project detail page now auto-polls every 30s via `gloo_timers::Interval`, so approved refunds/donations appear without manual refresh (`frontend/src/pages/project_detail.rs`)

**#7 - Role Assignment During Registration**
- Backend now respects the requested role for `Supporter`, `ProjectManager`, and `FinanceReviewer` (Administrator is blocked for security) (`backend/src/routes/auth_routes.rs`)
- Registration form now includes a role dropdown selector (`frontend/src/pages/login.rs`)
- Users can self-register as ProjectManager or FinanceReviewer
